THE MOTION ESTABLISHES THAT PLAINTIFF'S SECOND SET OF REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION WERE SERVED BY MAIL ON DEFENDANT ON FEBRUARY 21, 2018 AND NO RESPONSES HAVE BEEN SERVED. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AND PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 2033.280 (B), THE GENUINENESS OF ANY DOCUMENTS AND THE TRUTH OF ANY MATTERS SPECIFIED IN PLAINTIFF'S SECOND SET OF REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION ARE DEEMED ADMITTED. calendar.matters:
Defendant Mahmoud Razzaghi'S Motion For Reconsideration
Off Calendar Jun-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jeffrey Rosenfeld, Esq. 415-955-1155 x112 of Krnenberger Rosenfeld for Plaintiff.
Bart Blechschmidt, Esq. 619-850-0326 of Blechschmidt Law Firm for Defendant Mahmoud Razzaghi.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant to file a substitution of Attorney ASAP. Both sides Argue. The Court informs parties if Judge Jacskon confirms her ruling to deny the Continuance of the trial he will have to deny this motion. Parties are told to relay the message to Judge Jackson.

10:33 am the atter is recalled. Parties report Judge Jackson has denied the trial contiunace request. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT MAHMOUD RAZZAGHI's Motion For Reconsideration.

Denied. Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jun-25-2018 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jun-25-2018 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned by Dept. 501 for Prove-Up Hearing.
Judge: Leslie C. Nichols. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-717-9768. Email: casey.giselle@gmail.com.

Jeffrey M. Rosenfeld, Esq. and Liana Chen, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Mohammed Elahi, an individual, who was placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendant Mahmoud Razzaghi, an individual.

Plaintiff's complaint was filed on January 1, 2017.
Defendant's answer to plaintiff's complaint was filed on June 29, 2017.
Notice of Time & Place of Trial was served on August 17, 2017.
Statement of Damages was served on May 16, 2017 and filed on May 19, 2017.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Mohammed Elahi, an individual, and against defendant Mahmoud Razzaghi, an individual, as follows:

General Damages - $10,000.00.
Statutory Damages pursuant to Civil Code Sections 52 & 52.1 - $4,000.00.
Punitive Damages pursuant to Civil Code Section 3294 - $25,000.00.
Attorney Fees - $62,013.
Total - $101,013.00.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Motion For A Trial Continuance
Off Calendar Jun-21-2018 Text Ruling

Motion For A Trial Continuance
Off Calendar Jun-15-2018 Continued To Jun-21-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Mahmoud Razzaghi'S Motion For Reconsideration
Off Calendar Jun-11-2018 Continued To Jun-25-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jeffrey M. Rosenfeld, of Kronenberger Rosenfeld, Tel. 415.955.1155 x 112, for plaintiffs;
Mahmoud Razzaghi, pro per, Tel. 619.890.7909, for defendants, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. Plaintiff's counsel does not stipulate to the matter being heard by the Judge Pro Tem. DEFENDANT MAHMOUD RAZZAGHI'S Motion For Reconsideration is continued to June 25, 2018, at 9:30 a.m., in Department 302, Law & Motion. Judge Pro Tem to prepare report and recommendation. Judge Pro Tem: Stephen Zollman; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Establishing Defendant Mahmoud Razzaghis Admissions
Off Calendar May-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - Atty. Liana Chen of Kronenberger and Rosenfeld, Tel. No. 1-415-965-1156 appearing for the Plaintiff;
No other appearances on the matter.

Ruling - The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Mohammed Elahi's Motion For Order Establishing Defendant Mahmoud Razzaghis Admissions - No written opposition has been filed to the motion. The motion establishes that Plaintiff's Second Set of Requests for Admission were served by mail on Defendant on February 21, 2018 and no responses have been served. The motion is granted and pursuant to CCP section 2033.280 (b), the genuineness of any documents and the truth of any matters specified in Plaintiff's Second Set of Requests for Admission are deemed admitted. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro-tem: Sanford Kingsley; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not Reported. =302/jpt

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Feb-05-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ben Jakovljevic, Esq. of Adli Law Group PC, T:213.623.6546, counsel for defendant, and moving party, on Court Call;
Jeffrey M. Rosenfeld, Esq. of Kronenberger Rosenfeld LLP, T:415.955.1155, for plaintiff, on Court Call.

RULING: There being no opposition, or objection, the Court rules as follows:

MARINA MANOUKIAN'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT MAHMOUD RAZZAGHI is GRANTED. Counsel seeking to be relieved is required to prepare a proposed order granting the motion on the Judicial Council form. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Oct-16-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Karl Kronenberger, Esq. (415-955-1155 ext. 114) of Kronenberger for Plaintiff.
Brian Noh, Esq. (213-623-6546 ext. 3319) of Adli Law Group for Defendant through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces the reasons for the tentative ruling. Defendant argues material witness. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MAHMOUD RAZZAGHI's Motion For Change Of Venue.

Defendant Mahmoud Razzaghi's motion for change of venue is denied. Mr. Razzaghi failed to satisfy his burden to warrant transferring this case to Los Angeles. As stated at 3:576 in the Weil and Brown book, an "extensive factual showing is required" for a CCP 397(c) motion. Mr. Razzaghi's moving papers fall far short. Mr. Razzaghi only identifies one possible non-party witness who lives in or near Los Angeles and fails to show that her testimony is directly relevant to any of the contested issues in the case. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcripts) Order signed in open Court. (see scanned order for details) Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR No: 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)
